stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of Llama and Hugging Face indicates relevance within the large language model ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer, llama, and safetensors ecosystem.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered research agents struggle with verifiable source attribution, despite synthesizing information from numerous web sources.
Existing methods either rely on untrustworthy self-citation or use RAG without validating source accessibility or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel source attribution evaluation framework utilizing a reproducible AST parser to address this critical verification gap.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes common ML ecosystem components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its primary function is text generation.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
stories.author: Justine Calma
bullet_points: AI data centers are the essential physical infrastructure driving current AI ambitions.
The rapid expansion of these energy-intensive facilities is causing global friction.
Key concerns surrounding data center growth include strain on power grids, environmental impact, and community opposition.

stories.title: Apple reportedly has a deal to use Intel-made chips again
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly re-engaging with Intel for chip manufacturing.
This marks a potential reversal or collaboration following Apple's successful shift to Apple Silicon.
The agreement is described as 'preliminary' according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature by implementing more comprehensive source citation.
This change aims to improve transparency and verifiability within Google's generative AI search results.
The update represents a key course correction in how Google integrates and attributes information from its indexed sources.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method for video generation that allows joint control over character motion and camera trajectory.
It enables the transfer of character motion from a driving video into a novel scene.
The system provides per-frame control over intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters, enhancing cinematic control.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI co-mathematician,' a workbench designed to integrate AI agents into mathematical research.
The tool supports the entire research lifecycle, from ideation and literature review to computational exploration and theorem proving.
It provides a stateful, asynchronous workspace capable of managing uncertainty and tracking failed hypotheses during iterative mathematical workflows.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while allowing per-frame control over camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is built upon existing pretrained image-to-video diffusion models, conditioning them using scene depth and character information.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: Current LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback, are shown to be misleading.
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs reveals that the top models are statistically indistinguishable.
The study suggests that the global Bradley-Terry ranking fails because a large proportion of decisive votes cancel each other out.

stories.title: I Caught a Jailbreak Attack That Hides Inside Normal Conversations
stories.author: ayush_singh_9b0d83152be5b
bullet_points: The article discusses a novel jailbreak attack targeting AI systems.
The attack is designed to be stealthy, mimicking normal conversation patterns to evade detection.
This represents a significant security vulnerability in current AI conversational models.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access current information.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local generation on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within Ollama's open-model ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between proprietary models (Anthropic) and local, open-source deployment tools (Ollama).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon Prime Video is integrating a vertical, short-form video feed called 'Clips'.
This move positions Prime Video to compete with trends set by Netflix and Disney Plus.
The feed allows users to sample content and then access the full title via rental or purchase.

stories.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: A security vulnerability was exploited in Yarbo robot mowers, allowing them to be hijacked.
The hacking incident exposed sensitive user data, including GPS coordinates, Wi-Fi passwords, and emails.
Yarbo has issued a response promising to fix the security flaws in their robot mowers.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article discusses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible geometries.
The focus is on the difficulty of manufacturing movable qubits.
This points to ongoing hurdles in advanced quantum hardware development.

stories.title: Sony says "efficient" AI tools will lead to even more games flooding the market
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Sony anticipates that 'efficient' AI tools will result in a significant increase in the number of games released.
Despite the rise of AI-driven content creation, Sony emphasizes that human artists must remain central to the development process.
The news touches upon the industry trend of AI integration in game development and its impact on market saturation.

stories.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A cyberattack has disrupted the Canvas learning platform across multiple schools and colleges.
The disruption was severe enough to force institutions to postpone year-end examinations.
The incident highlights the critical reliance of educational systems on cloud-based learning infrastructure.

stories.title: Elon Musk faces criminal probe in France after ignoring summons in X case
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Elon Musk is facing a criminal probe in France.
The probe relates to an issue concerning the platform X.
France is threatening criminal charges if Musk fails to appear for questioning.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has isolated expert capacity.
Suggests that uniform random routing can maintain high accuracy, indicating that rigid per-layer expert allocation might be unnecessary.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs struggle to generate valid, challenging, and novel mathematical problems needed for advanced training.
The proposed method, VHG, addresses the limitations of current approaches (human dependency or naive self-play).
VHG utilizes a 'verifier-backed' mechanism to ensure generated problems are mathematically sound, mitigating reward hacking.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Proposes a framework for 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' using scenario-based audits as deployment evidence.
Highlights the necessity of fixing several parameters (scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) to ensure score validity in the absence of labels.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user rather than an expert.
The proposed advancement aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors, reducing latency and unnecessary retrieval rounds.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current MMDG research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of optimizing LLM agents for long-horizon decision-making, where current methods are too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework that integrates explicit trajectory-level strategies into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making sequences in LLM agents.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional design where each transformer layer has a rigid, isolated expert set.
Suggests that decoupling expert allocation from layer depth might improve efficiency, based on routing probe results.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in LLMs: their inability to generate valid, challenging, and novel problems for advanced training.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verifiers to ensure the mathematical validity of generated problems, overcoming issues with naive self-play.
This work is relevant to the future of autonomous scientific research and improving the robustness of LLMs.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of optimizers in Large Language Model (LLM) training, specifically during finetuning.
The study observes that using the same optimizer as pretraining during full finetuning leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during Supervised Finetuning (SFT).

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployments.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current retrieval-augmented agents, which often treat retrieval as a 'black box'.
Existing methods mimic novice search behavior (iterative querying) rather than expert knowledge navigation.
The proposed work aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors, reducing unnecessary retrieval rounds and latency.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current MMDG research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are heavily skewed towards action recognition, overlooking other critical real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) where only a subset of capabilities is needed.
Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models are proposed as a solution to activate only relevant components for specific tasks.
The research tackles a practical limitation of MoEs: performance degradation when inference is strictly restricted to a subset of experts.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs excel at solving math/science problems but struggle to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
The paper introduces VHG, a new method to generate hard problems for LLMs.
VHG addresses limitations in existing methods, which either require human experts or suffer from invalid outputs via naive self-play.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployment audits.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can be considered valid deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents due to purely reactive methods.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to integrate explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision sequences.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new RL approach for training recursive agents.
Recursive agents can spawn and delegate sub-tasks to new instances of themselves recursively.
This architecture enables inference-time scaling for longer contexts and tackling more complex problems via divide-and-conquer.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in current LLM research: the lack of controlled, scalable environments for studying reasoning.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two critical axes of difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to systematically improve long-horizon reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a new framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents, a non-trivial problem in current MAS designs.

stories.title: Google broke reCAPTCHA for de-googled Android users
stories.author: anonymousiam
bullet_points: Google's reCAPTCHA system is reportedly failing or being bypassed for users on de-googled Android devices.
The story touches upon the ongoing tension between Google's services and users opting out of Google's ecosystem.
Related discussions suggest Google is evolving its fraud defense, potentially linking it to other technologies like WEI.
